change_summary: Solver mis-classified 115/116 as rejects; gold criterion is explicit (aligned atoms saying 'Exactly this' do not reject). Genuine reading error.

Solver's stricter reading of 422's 'broke down quite easily within a year' is defensible under 'specific hardware issue named'. Add concept-level clause: whole-device failure events within a short ownership window qualify alongside component-specific failures.
Solver only enumerated 2 of 6 subtree atoms; 516 (lid-angle sensor) and 517 (acoustic engineering) were missed. Gold criterion internally consistent.
Gold criterion explicitly excludes 'bare complaints adjacent to workaround discussion'; 487 (powertop complaint) and 493 (hedged 'haven't researched') fall under the exclusion. Solver over-counted.
Gold criterion: 'explicit procedural recipe or instruction'. 507 (repair-shop recommendation) and 509 ('week or two' wait-advice) meet it. Solver was too strict. Genuine reading error.
Solver LOW confidence with only 1 atom enumerated; did not enumerate the 14-node longest chain. Per Phase 6 protocol, LOW+incomplete-enum flagged, gold retained.
Solver LOW confidence with 0 atoms enumerated — protocol violation (C6 requires non-empty enumeration). Gold retained; flagged.
Solver LOW confidence with 0 atoms enumerated — protocol violation (C5 requires non-empty enumeration). Gold retained; flagged.
Scope locator 'largest' resolved to hn_comment_219 (education, 234 nodes) but question content matched hn_comment_1 (IPv6 objections, 218 nodes). Fixed: 'largest' → 'second-largest'.
Scope locator 'second-largest' resolved to hn_comment_1 but question content matched hn_comment_219 (education). Fixed: 'second-largest' → 'largest'.

Scope locator 'non-root with most direct replies' resolved to hn_comment_2 (14 replies) but content matched hn_comment_3 (13 replies). Fixed: replaced with content-paraphrase locator 'depth-three comment that defends NAT as dual-layer security'.
Scope locator 'largest sub-discussion' resolved to hn_comment_219 but hn_comment_5 is in hn_comment_1. Fixed: 'largest' → 'second-largest'.

arccy (728) cites enterprise exhaustion but body-majority thrust is ad hominem. Added disambiguation clause: replies whose primary rhetorical purpose is personal attack do NOT count even when citing enterprise scenarios as supporting evidence. Gold count (7) held.

Solver chose 'Middle third by upvote' because n6a5pab (rank 5) sits at the top of the middle third, and the solver weighted all three replies equally rather than noting that 2 of 3 are in the top third. Added concept-level disambiguation clause to criterion_definition: 'Predominantly = the modal tier when each subset member is assigned to its tier; ties broken by the tier containing the plurality.' Stem unchanged — the word 'predominantly' already appears. Gold answer remains 'Top third by upvote' because 2 of 3 are in the top third.
